reports.title: Iran Sends Diplomat to Pakistan, Refuses to Meet US Envoys
reports.subtitle: A ceasefire without a deal: both sides need the talks to succeed and both need to be seen not needing them.

reports.title: Appeals Court Kills Trump's Asylum Ban. The Supreme Court Is Next.
reports.subtitle: A 2-1 ruling says the president cannot rewrite immigration law by proclamation. Trump's path to SCOTUS is now the point.

reports.title: One Year of Trump Tariffs: The Numbers Are In
reports.subtitle: The Budget Lab at Yale finds 460,000 jobs lost and $1,700 per household per year. The administration says this proves the policy is working.

reports.title: The Big Tobacco Moment That Isn't
reports.subtitle: Two landmark verdicts against Meta and Google have parents lobbying Congress. The companies will appeal. The real question is whether the courtroom victory can survive Section 230 and the First Amendment.

reports.title: Meta Bets Billions on AWS Chips While Cutting 8,000 Jobs
reports.subtitle: The agentic AI era needs CPUs more than GPUs, and that changes who controls the infrastructure of artificial intelligence.

reports.title: The Federal Government Just Told States They Cannot Regulate AI Bias
reports.subtitle: The DOJ's decision to join xAI's suit against Colorado isn't about Musk. It's a constitutional claim that anti-discrimination mandates in AI are themselves unconstitutional discrimination.

reports.title: Trump Owes $159 Billion in Tariff Refunds. He Is Telling Companies Not to Collect.
reports.subtitle: The Supreme Court struck down the IEEPA tariffs, CBP opened a refund portal, and Trump responded by publicly threatening companies that use it. The rule of law is now a political risk calculation.

reports.title: Britain Shot Down a Russian Drone Over Ukraine. Or It Didn't.
reports.subtitle: Romania says the RAF engaged and destroyed a Russian drone over Ukrainian territory. The UK MoD says the jets returned to base without firing. The gap between those two accounts is where the actual escalation lives.

reports.title: The Man Trump Picked to Run the Fed Says He Won't Do What Trump Wants
reports.subtitle: Kevin Warsh's confirmation hearing produced the predictable pledges of independence. The predictable part is the problem: every Fed nominee says this, and the chair who actually defies a sitting president has not yet existed.

reports.title: Turkey Bans Social Media for Under-15s. The Global Wave Just Got Bigger.
reports.subtitle: A school shooting triggered the law. Erdogan's political agenda is sustaining it. The question is whether any country that bans children from social media is actually protecting them or just building surveillance infrastructure with parental consent.
